title: Noah Kahan joins Fenway Music Hall of Fame after historic four-night run
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Noah Kahan was inducted into the Fenway Music Hall of Fame after a historic four-night run at Fenway Park.
Kahan joined Hall of Fame inductees Billy Joel, Paul McCartney, and the Zac Brown Band during his final show on Saturday.
Massachusetts Governor Maura Healey declared the date of Kahan's final show 'Noah Kahan Day' in celebration of his achievement.

title: AG: Mass. to receive $730,000 from Cash App in alleged deception settlement
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Massachusetts to receive $730,000 from Cash App in a $45 million multistate settlement for alleged deceptive practices.
Block Inc., owner of Cash App, allegedly misled users about safety and allowed practices that facilitated fraud.
Settlement requires Cash App to improve user protection, cease false safety claims, and enhance customer service support.

title: Tyngsborough man may face charges after allegedly taking police boat, posing as officer
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A 63-year-old Tyngsborough man allegedly took a police boat on Lake Mascuppic, impersonating an officer to enforce boating rules.
Police responded and found the man operating the boat with emergency lights; he returned it after being instructed by officers.
Tyngsborough police intend to summon the man for impersonating an officer; a clerk-magistrate will assess evidence for charging.

title: Photos: Stunning views of Sail Boston 250
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Over 60 tall ships and military vessels from more than 20 countries participated in Sail Boston 2026.
The USS Constitution led the Parade of Sail through Boston Harbor, drawing thousands of spectators.
Vessels, including the Columbia Arc Gloria and Polish Dar Mlodziezy, are docked at Boston's waterfront locations.

title: Red Sox draft SS Jake Schaffner with No. 20 pick
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox drafted Jake Schaffner, a shortstop from UNC, with the No. 20 overall pick in the 2026 MLB Draft.
Schaffner, ranked 75th by MLB.com, hit .356 with 6 home runs, 50 RBI, and stole 26 bases in 2026.
Despite Schaffner's .987 fielding percentage at shortstop, MLB.com suggests a potential move to second base in pro ball.

title: Sail Boston 2026: The Grand Parade of Sail is rooted in rich history and tradition in the Commonwealth
author: Laurie Fullerton
bullets: The Grand Parade of Sail, led by the USS Constitution, showcased historic ships in Boston, marking a rare gathering opportunity.
Spectators gathered from Castle Island to Piers Park, honoring maritime history and noted shipbuilders like Donald McKay.
The event featured ships including the Grand Banks replica Bluenose Two; volunteers emphasized educational and historical significance.

title: Brayan Bello named midseason AL ‘Cy Yuk’ winner by The Athletic for poor first-half performance
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Brayan Bello, a Boston Red Sox pitcher, was named the AL 'Cy Yuk' winner by The Athletic for his poor performance.
Bello posted a 10.35 ERA in eight starts before being demoted to Triple-A, with batters hitting .365 against him.
Despite relief success, Bello remains in Triple-A while the Red Sox recalled Eduardo Rivera to start in his place.

title: Red Sox face all too familiar question heading into MLB trade deadline: Buy or sell?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox on seven-game winning streak, two games from AL wild-card spot, prompting buy/sell dilemma at trade deadline.
Boston's previous indecision at 2024 and 2025 trade deadlines led to underwhelming results; clarity needed in 2026.
Craig Breslow, facing job scrutiny, must decide if recent team improvement warrants retaining assets or acquiring talent.

title: Review & Setlist: Shakira delivers high-energy nostalgia and new beginnings at TD Garden
author: Maddie Browning
bullets: Shakira performed at TD Garden in Boston as part of her Las Mujeres Ya No Lloran World Tour, delivering a high-energy show.
The concert featured a mix of nostalgia and new songs, including hits like 'Hips Don't Lie' and 'Whenever, Wherever'.
Shakira's performance highlighted her resilience and artistic evolution since releasing her Grammy-winning album 'Las Mujeres Ya No Lloran'.

title: Healey announces tax credit awards to build almost 700 new homes in six Mass. cities
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Maura Healey announces $18.6 million in tax credits for 662 new homes in six Massachusetts Gateway Cities.
Projects involve converting historic buildings in cities like Fall River and Lawrence into residential units.
The initiative follows a 2023 tax relief package, tripling the Housing Development Incentive Program's annual authorization.
